evals.prompt: hey so I just launched this vitamin C serum on Shopify and my current product page is basically just the manufacturer spec sheet copy-pasted in lol. it's a 30ml glass dropper bottle, 15% L-ascorbic acid, hyaluronic acid, vitamin E, vegan, cruelty-free, retails at $38. my customers are mostly women 25-40 who want brighter skin without a 10-step routine. I want the full thing — title, description, bullets, meta, the works. target keyword is 'vitamin c face serum'. tone should be clean and aspirational, not clinical
evals.expected_output: SEO-optimized title (60-80 chars with keyword), 300-500 word description following hook → solution → feature-benefit blocks → trust → scenario → CTA flow, 5-7 benefit-first bullet points, meta description 150-160 chars, emotional hooks / power words, all in an aspirational-not-clinical tone with 2-3% keyword density.
expectations: Title is 60-80 characters and contains 'vitamin c' or 'Vitamin C'
Description is 300-500 words
Description opens with a pain point or desired outcome, not the product name
Each feature (L-ascorbic acid, hyaluronic acid, vitamin E) is translated into a customer benefit
Bullet points section has 5-7 items, each leading with a benefit
Meta description is 150-160 characters and contains the primary keyword
Tone is aspirational and sensory, not clinical or medical-sounding
No unverifiable superlatives like 'best serum' without proof

evals.prompt: I sell ergonomic office chairs on Shopify, this is our hero product at $429. specs: mesh back, adjustable lumbar support, 4D armrests, 135 degree recline, weight capacity 300lb, BIFMA certified, 5 year warranty. our audience is remote workers 30-50 who have back pain from cheap chairs. I don't want it to sound too techy — more like 'we understand your pain, here's the fix'. can you write the product description and bullets? also need a good title, we want to rank for 'ergonomic office chair'
evals.expected_output: Product title optimized for 'ergonomic office chair', description 300-500 words that leads with the back-pain problem (not specs), translates each spec into a user benefit (mesh=breathability, lumbar=targeted support, recline=pressure relief), 5-7 benefit-first bullets, meta description. Confident and empathetic tone, not overly technical.
expectations: Title contains 'ergonomic' and 'office chair' and is 60-80 characters
Description opens with the back-pain problem or relief outcome, not a spec list
Mesh back is connected to breathability or temperature comfort
Adjustable lumbar is connected to personalized support or pain relief
BIFMA certification and 5-year warranty appear as trust signals
Bullets lead with benefits, not spec names
Meta description is 150-160 characters
Tone is empathetic and confident, not a spec dump

evals.prompt: ok so I make soy candles by hand in my garage in portland and I sell on etsy. this one is lavender + eucalyptus, 8oz tin, 45 hour burn time, cotton wick, hand poured in small batches. priced at $24. I want something that feels warm and artisan-y, like you're buying from a real person not amazon. keyword is 'lavender soy candle'. keep the description shortish, maybe under 350 words? I need the title and bullets and meta too
evals.expected_output: Etsy-friendly title with 'lavender soy candle', description under 350 words with warm artisan tone and sensory language, emphasis on handmade/small-batch/Portland origin, 5-7 bullets covering scent, burn time, materials, handmade, and gift-worthiness, meta description 150-160 chars.
expectations: Title contains 'lavender soy candle'
Description is under 350 words
Description uses sensory language for the scent (lavender + eucalyptus)
Handmade / small-batch / Portland origin are woven into the narrative, not just listed
Tone feels personal and artisan, not corporate
Bullets include burn time (45 hours) as a benefit, not just a spec
Meta description is 150-160 characters and contains the keyword
No filler phrases like 'high quality' or 'amazing product'
